meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Break out of tutorial hell by building a user onboarding flow. A beginner-friendly guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first project.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop watching and start building. By engineering a custom user onboarding flow, you bridge the gap between theory and product. This project forces you to manage state, handle user inputs, and persist data—the fundamental building blocks of every production-grade SaaS application today.
content.intro:
You’ve watched the hours of boilerplate tutorials, yet your screen remains blank. The cycle of passive consumption is a trap that keeps you from the only thing that matters: shipping. It is time to stop being a spectator and start being an architect.

User onboarding is the perfect crucible for your first real-world project. It touches every critical part of an application—state management, database interactions, and UI responsiveness. By building a flow that actually guides a user, you transform from a tutorial-follower into a builder who understands how software is actually constructed.

steps.title: Architecting the Flow
steps.description: Define the user journey. Map out the steps from signup to the 'Aha!' moment. This creates a blueprint for your code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Engineer. Design a 3-step onboarding flow for a SaaS app. Define the state transitions and required data schema for each step.

steps.title: State Management Setup
steps.description: Implement a robust way to track progress across the multi-step form without losing user data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React hook for managing multi-step form state using Zustand. Include validation logic for each step.

steps.title: Dynamic UI Components
steps.description: Build the visual interface. Focus on clean, modular components that handle loading states and input errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable 'OnboardingStep' wrapper component with transition animations and progress tracking.

steps.title: Persistence Layer
steps.description: Save user progress to a database so they can return later. This is the difference between a prototype and an app.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Supabase service function to upsert onboarding data based on the current user ID.

steps.title: Final Integration
steps.description: Connect the UI to the backend and test the full user journey from start to finish.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the entire flow integration. Check for race conditions and ensure data persistence triggers correctly on every step completion.

pro_tips: Build for the 'Back' button: Beginners always forget that users will try to go back. Build your state machine to handle reverse navigation first.
Mock the backend early: Don't wait for your database to be perfect. Use local storage to simulate persistence until your schema is finalized.
Focus on the 'Aha!' moment: Your onboarding should deliver value within 30 seconds. If it takes longer, cut the steps.
Log every event: Add simple console logs or analytics triggers for every step. It’s the easiest way to debug user friction.
Don't over-engineer the UI: Spend 20% of your time on styling and 80% on the data flow. Nobody cares about your custom CSS if the data doesn't save.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is live, implement a 'Dashboard' view that pulls the data you just collected. This closes the loop and gives you a fully functional MVP.
content.conversion_block: Stop watching others build and start building your own future. You have the tools, the roadmap, and the intent. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
